name: region_flush_idempotent_external_body
reason: `lemma_if_no_outstanding_writes_to_region_then_flush_is_idempotent` is `external_body` — single-region flush idempotency is trusted without proof; the multi-region lemma's correctness entirely depends on this unverified assumption

name: flush_outstanding_write_takes_new_value
code:
proof fn phi_2_flush_outstanding_write_takes_new_value(b: PersistentMemoryByte, val: u8)
    requires
        b.outstanding_write == Some(val),
    ensures
        b.flush().state_at_last_flush == val,
        b.flush().state_at_last_flush != b.state_at_last_flush || val == b.state_at_last_flush,
{
}
reason: After flushing a byte with an outstanding write, the old `state_at_last_flush` is permanently overwritten — tests that flush commits the pending value and loses the previous state

name: flush_region_preserves_length
reason: Flushing a region should not change its size — if map altered the sequence length, indexing after flush would be invalid

name: no_outstanding_writes_after_flush
reason: After flushing all bytes, every byte should have `outstanding_write == None` — if flush left outstanding writes, a second flush could change state, violating crash consistency

name: no_outstanding_writes_in_empty_range
reason: An empty range should vacuously have no outstanding writes — if the range predicate were off-by-one (using `<=` instead of `<`), empty ranges would require checking a byte
